title: HostEventEnvelopeTransportV1
description: Transport-level envelope that a host sends to Vega. Host thin, Vega thick: the host only validates transport shape; canonical normalization and memory intelligence stay in Vega.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier generated by the host.

surface.description: Host surface that produced the event (e.g. claude, codex, cursor, opencode, hermes, api, cli). Free string at transport level; Vega normalizes to canonical values.

session_id.description: Session identifier scoped by the host.

thread_id.description: Optional thread identifier. Null when the host does not know it.

project.description: Project name. Null when the host does not know it.

cwd.description: Current working directory at event time. Null when the host does not know it.

host_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the event was produced by the host.

role.description: Role of the event sender (e.g. user, assistant, system, tool). Free string at transport level; Vega normalizes to canonical values.

event_type.description: Type of the event (e.g. message, tool_call, tool_result, decision, state_change). Free string at transport level; Vega normalizes to canonical values.

payload.description: Opaque JSON object containing the event payload. The host structures the raw event; Vega interprets it.

artifacts.description: Array of artifact references attached to the event.

source_kind.description: Optional provenance hint describing where this envelope originated.

redacted.description: Whether the host applied redaction to the payload.

categories.description: List of safety categories applied by the host (e.g. secret, pii).

size_bytes.description: Optional size of the artifact in bytes.
